neighborhoods.name: Old San Juan / Viejo San Juan
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO-listed historic district with cobbled streets, El Morro fortress, the cathedral, and vibrant cafes and galleries on Calle Fortaleza

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial mansions and apartment conversions

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of San Juan. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Juan

neighborhoods.name: Condado
neighborhoods.description: Beachfront condo strip along Ashford Avenue with restaurants, hotels, and the Laguna del Condado, popular with mainland transplants and retirees
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to San Juan's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Juan

neighborhoods.name: Santurce
neighborhoods.description: Interior arts district with La Placita de Santurce, the Museum of Art of Puerto Rico, and significantly lower rents than Condado
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Juan

neighborhoods.name: Miramar
neighborhoods.description: Quiet historic neighborhood between Old San Juan and Condado with restored 1920s-30s mansions, embassies, and a slower pace

housing.predominantType: Restored mansions, condos, and small apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living San Juan
